title: [InstCombine] Remove dead phi web
summary:
The developers generalized a linear dead PHI chain detection into a robust dead PHI web elimination. They replaced the recursive `isDeadPHICycle` function (which only handled simple linear chains) with a new iterative `foldDeadPhiWeb` that:

1. Uses worklist-based DFS to collect all interconnected PHIs in a web
2. Validates that every PHI user within the web is also a PHI (ensuring the entire web is dead)
3. Implements a size limit (16 PHIs) to avoid excessive compile-time on large webs
4. Bulk-replaces all web PHIs with poison and erases them atomically

Key generalization insights:
- Move from single-chain to graph-based detection using visited sets and worklists
- Replace recursive validation with iterative collection followed by batch removal
- Maintain safety with early-exit conditions while handling more complex patterns
- Integrate early in the PHI visitor to clean dead webs before other transformations

This pattern demonstrates how to evolve single-case optimizations into general graph transformations by systematically collecting connected components and validating global properties before removal.
